SEARCH
Package edu.yu.cs.com3810.gateway

Class ApiGateway

java.lang.Object
edu.yu.cs.com3810.gateway.ApiGateway
public class ApiGateway extends Object
The API Gateway is the single point of entry for all user-facing requests.

It exposes REST-style methods corresponding to the operations a user can perform via the web or mobile app. The gateway is responsible for:

  • Discovering and tracking the current leader coordinator.
  • Forwarding all user requests to the leader and returning responses.
  • Shielding the user from the internal cluster topology.

The gateway does not process business logic itself — it is a thin routing and translation layer between the user interface and the coordinator cluster.

    Constructs the API Gateway.

  • Constructor Details

    • ApiGateway

      public ApiGateway(List<String> coordinatorAddresses)
      Constructs the API Gateway.

      On construction the gateway does not yet know who the leader is. Call start() to begin polling for the leader before serving requests.

      Parameters:
      coordinatorAddresses - the network addresses of all known coordinator nodes, used for leader discovery polling

  • Method Details

    • start

      public void start()
      Starts the API Gateway by polling the coordinator cluster until a leader is elected and identified. Must complete before any user requests are served.

      Polls any coordinator node — all nodes can answer "who is the leader?" regardless of their own role, as per the system design.

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Gracefully shuts down the API Gateway.

    • refreshLeader

      public void refreshLeader()
      Polls the coordinator cluster to refresh the current leader address. Called on startup and whenever a request fails due to a stale leader reference, indicating a failover has occurred.

    • getCurrentLeaderAddress

      public String getCurrentLeaderAddress()
      Returns the network address of the current leader coordinator.
      Returns:
      the address (host:port or node ID) of the current leader

    • getAllDevices

      public List<Device> getAllDevices()
      Retrieves all devices registered with the Smart Home system. Forwards the request to the leader and returns the resulting device list.
      Returns:
      a list of all registered Device objects

    • getDevice

      public Device getDevice(String deviceId)
      Retrieves a single device by its ID.
      Parameters:
      deviceId - the ID of the device to retrieve
      Returns:
      the Device with the given ID, or null if not found

    • sendCommand

      public CommandStatus sendCommand(Command command)
      Sends a command to a specific device.

      The gateway forwards the command to the current leader coordinator. If the leader has changed (detected via a failed request), the gateway calls refreshLeader() and retries once before returning an error.

      Parameters:
      command - the command to send to the target device
      Returns:
      the CommandStatus result returned by the leader

    • addAutomationRule

      public boolean addAutomationRule(AutomationRule rule)
      Submits a new automation rule to the system. Forwards the rule to the leader coordinator, which stores it and notifies the Automation Hub.
      Parameters:
      rule - the automation rule to create
      Returns:
      true if the rule was successfully added, false otherwise

    • removeAutomationRule

      public boolean removeAutomationRule(String ruleId)
      Removes an existing automation rule. Forwards the removal request to the leader coordinator.
      Parameters:
      ruleId - the ID of the rule to remove
      Returns:
      true if the rule was found and removed, false otherwise

    • toggleAutomationRule

      public boolean toggleAutomationRule(String ruleId, boolean active)
      Enables or disables an existing automation rule without removing it. Forwards the toggle request to the leader coordinator, which publishes an AutomationRuleMessage with operation TOGGLE to the AUTOMATION topic.
      Parameters:
      ruleId - the ID of the rule to toggle
      active - true to enable the rule, false to disable it
      Returns:
      true if the rule was found and updated, false otherwise

    • getAutomationRules

      public List<AutomationRule> getAutomationRules()
      Retrieves all automation rules currently active in the system.
      Returns:
      a list of all AutomationRule instances
